orchestrator.description: Julia-Orchestrator is Julia's central brain. It polls the Bridge for messages, generates replies via Claude Haiku (with GPT-4o fallback), manages memory, and delegates complex tasks to Cowork-MCP.

openclaw.description: OpenClaw is Julia's Telegram gateway — it receives messages from Telegram, forwards them to the Bridge, and delivers replies back to users.

bridge.description: The Julia-Bridge (port 3001) is the central message hub. It exposes MCP tools and REST endpoints that connect the Orchestrator, OpenClaw, and Frontend.

backend.description: REST API running in Docker with Prisma + PostgreSQL. Stores tasks, memories, letters, logs, and usage data for the entire system.

database.description: PostgreSQL database storing persistent state — tasks, memories, letters, conversation logs, and AI usage metrics.

user.description: The human operator. Communicates via Telegram (through OpenClaw) or the web Dashboard (through Julia-Web).

cowork.description: Cowork-MCP (port 3003) wraps the Anthropic Claude API as MCP tools, giving Julia a powerful second brain for complex reasoning, code review, and multimodal analysis.

frontend.description: Julia-Web Dashboard (port 3002). Built with React + Vite + Tailwind. Features AI chat (GPT-4o via Vercel AI SDK), DevOps panel, and this architecture view.

julia-relay.description: OpenClaw skill that forwards Telegram messages to the Julia-Bridge via HTTP POST and polls for replies. The core routing skill that connects Telegram to Julia.

bash-linux.description: OpenClaw skill providing shell command execution. Allows Julia to run system commands, check service status, and perform operational tasks.

openclaw-expert.description: Orchestrator skill for managing OpenClaw configuration, troubleshooting connection issues, and understanding the Telegram gateway architecture.

memory-systems.description: Orchestrator skill for memory extraction and retrieval. Categorizes moments as STORY, FEELING, MOMENT, WISH, or REFLECTION and stores them long-term.

security-auditor.description: Ambient security agent that scans for vulnerabilities, exposed secrets, and misconfigurations across the multi-agent system.

postgres-expert.description: Skill for PostgreSQL database management — schema migrations, query optimization, and Prisma ORM guidance.

ui-ux-pro-max.description: Design skill for frontend UI/UX decisions — layout patterns, accessibility, responsive design, and Tailwind styling.

exa-search.description: Research skill using Exa semantic search API for finding relevant web content, academic papers, and documentation.

python-pro.description: Python development skill for data processing, scripting, and building Python-based tools and agents.

health-checker.description: Ambient agent running via PM2. Monitors service health, detects failures, and reports issues to shared-findings for cross-agent awareness.

docs-agent.description: Ambient agent that detects undocumented git commits every 12 hours and alerts via Telegram when documentation drifts from code.

security-agent.description: Ambient security agent generating daily reports, scanning for exposed secrets, and maintaining a security baseline.

adhd-agent.description: System hygiene agent running every 4 hours via macOS LaunchAgent. Scans for duplicate skills, dead agents, and orphaned configurations.

architecture-agent.description: Ambient agent that scans the live system topology (PM2 processes, skills, incidents) and generates the dynamic architecture graph.

pm2.description: PM2 process manager supervising all ambient agents. Handles auto-restart, log rotation, and process monitoring.

docker.description: Docker containers running the backend REST API and PostgreSQL database. Managed via docker-compose.

shared-findings.description: Cross-agent communication backbone (incidents.json). Agents write findings here; other agents read them for situational awareness.
